title: quercus aliena var. urticifolia
intro: Quercus aliena var. urticifolia, also known as the Japanese pedunculate oak, is a deciduous tree native to East Asia, specifically in China, Japan, and Korea. The plant is rich in flavonoids, tannins, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. It has been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, and has shown potential health benefits in reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and acting as an antioxidant. The herbal preparations of Quercus aliena var. urticifolia include decoctions, infusions, and extracts, which can be used to make teas, capsules, and topical creams for various health applications.
